summary: Tested Budget App backend (13 pytest cases) and frontend e2e. Backend: 12/13 PASS. Frontend: all core flows PASS (budget, add expense, metrics calc, table, delete, charts, Excel/PDF export, Rupiah formatting). Only failure is file upload to object storage (401 Unauthorized from emergent storage API).

critical.issue: Object storage init fails with 401 Unauthorized from https://integrations.emergentagent.com/objstore/api/v1/storage/init. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY may not be valid for object storage service, or storage endpoint requires different auth. This blocks the 'Bukti Pembayaran' file upload feature entirely.

minor.issue: Returns 200 on create instead of 201 (acceptable but non-standard)
Auto-creates a default 0 budget on first GET (side-effect in GET handler) — should be a pure read

ui_bugs.issue: State `showExpenseModal` and handler `setShowExpenseModal(true)` are set on 'Tambah Pengeluaran' and Edit buttons, but no <Dialog> component is rendered for it. Editing works only because the right-side quick form is auto-populated; on mobile or when form is scrolled away, clicking Edit gives no visible feedback. The 'Tambah Pengeluaran' button only resets the form with no modal popup.
Upload fails silently from user POV with toast 'Gagal mengupload file' due to backend 500. Root cause is object storage 401 (see backend critical).

integration_issues.issue: End-to-end file upload broken; expenses can be created without bukti but cannot attach proof

issues: 'Atur Budget' button gets partially hidden behind sonner toast notifications in top-right corner; consider moving toaster to top-center or bottom
Monthly bar chart Y-axis shows raw numbers (e.g., 160000) instead of Rupiah-formatted ticks
Pie chart label uses entry.kategori but in single-category case label overlaps the pie

action_items: Investigate why EMERGENT_LLM_KEY returns 401 on storage/init — verify key validity for object storage service, or fix auth header (the init request sends emergent_key in body; the storage service may expect a different header/format)
Remove unused `showExpenseModal` state OR add a proper <Dialog> wrapping the expense form so edit/add buttons open a modal as intended
Format chart Y-axis ticks with Rupiah/short notation (e.g., Rp 160K)
GET /api/budget should not insert a default record (side-effect); return zeros instead
critical_code_review_comments: showExpenseModal is dead state — opening modal does nothing because no Dialog uses it
GET /api/budget mutates DB on first call (anti-pattern)
Bare `except:` in get_expense_stats (line 285) swallows all errors silently
No Pydantic validation for negative amounts in Budget/Expense; user can enter negative nominal
Frontend imports `xlsx` package but never uses it (dead dep — exports happen server-side)
ExpenseUpdate omits bukti_path/bukti_filename, so users can't change/clear attachment on edit
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed expenses and clean up via fixture
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all CRUD + dashboard + stats + exports + upload. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Upload test will fail until object storage auth is fixed. Frontend looks correct visually; main blocker is the 401 from object storage init endpoint.
rca of the issue: POST /api/upload → backend calls put_object → calls init_storage → POST to https://integrations.emergentagent.com/objstore/api/v1/storage/init with body {emergent_key: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY} → returns 401 Unauthorized. Either the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in backend/.env is not authorized for object storage, the storage init API contract changed (auth header instead of body), or the service is unreachable for this key. Backend then raises 500 to the client and the React dropzone shows toast 'Gagal mengupload file'. Mitigation: web_search the latest object storage init contract or contact emergent support; alternatively use local file storage as a fallback while object storage is unavailable.
